briefdescription.para: An opaque CAN network interface type.
An opaque CAN timer type.
An opaque CAN frame receiver type.

memberdef.definition: typedef int can_timer_func_t(const struct timespec *tp, void *data)
memberdef.name: can_timer_func_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CAN timer callback function, invoked by a CAN timer when the time is updated, or by a CAN network interface when the time at which the next timer triggers is updated.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the current time.
a pointer to user-specified data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, implementations SHOULD set the error number with set_errnum().

memberdef.definition: typedef int can_recv_func_t(const struct can_msg *msg, void *data)
memberdef.name: can_recv_func_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CAN receive callback function, invoked by a CAN frame receiver when a frame is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the received CAN frame.
a pointer to user-specified data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, implementations SHOULD set the error number with set_errnum().

memberdef.definition: typedef int can_send_func_t(const struct can_msg *msg, uint_least8_t bus_id, void *data)
memberdef.name: can_send_func_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CAN send callback function, invoked by a CAN network interface when a frame needs to be sent.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the CAN frame to be sent.
a CAN bus identifier.
a pointer to user-specified data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, implementations SHOULD set the error number with set_errnum().

briefdescription.para: Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the can_net_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the can_net_t structure.
Creates a new CAN network interface.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a memory allocator. If alloc is NULL, the default memory allocator is used.
the active CAN bus identifier.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a CAN network interface.
Returns a pointer to the allocator used to allocate the CAN network interface.
Retrieves the current time of a CAN network interface.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
the address at which to store the current time (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the current time of a CAN network interface.

detaileddescription.para: This MAY invoke one or more CAN timer callback functions.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
a pointer to the current time.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code set by the first failed CAN timer callback function can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the callback function invoked when the time at which the next CAN timer triggers is updated.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
the address at which to store a pointer to the callback function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the callback function invoked when the time at which the next CAN timer triggers is updated.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
a pointer to the function to be invoked by can_net_send().
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: Receives a CAN frame with a network interface on the given bus.

detaileddescription.para: If the frame is received on the active bus, it is processed with the corresponding receiver(s). Otherwise, the frame is ignored. This function MAY invoke one or more CAN frame receiver callback functions.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
a pointer to the CAN frame to be processed.
a CAN bus identifier.

simplesect.para: the number of processed frames, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code set by the first failed CAN frame receiver callback function can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Sends a CAN frame from a network interface.

detaileddescription.para: This function invokes the callback function set by can_net_set_send_func().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
a pointer to the CAN frame to be sent.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code set by the CAN send callback function can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the callback function used to send CAN frames from a network interface.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
the address at which to store a pointer to the callback function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the callback function used to send CAN frames from a network interface.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
a pointer to the function to be invoked by can_net_send().
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: Returns the active CAN bus identifier of the network interface.
Sets the active CAN bus of the network interface.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network interface.
a CAN bus identifier.

briefdescription.para: Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the can_timer_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the can_timer_t structure.
Creates a new CAN timer.
Destroys a CAN timer.
Returns a pointer to the allocator used to allocate the CAN timer.
Retrieves the callback function invoked when a CAN timer is triggered.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN timer.
the address at which to store a pointer to the callback function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the callback function invoked when a CAN timer is triggered.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN timer.
a pointer to the function to be invoked by can_net_set_time().
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

memberdef.argsstring: (can_timer_t *timer, can_net_t *net, const struct timespec *start, const struct timespec *interval)
memberdef.name: can_timer_start

briefdescription.para: Starts a CAN timer and registers it with a network interface.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN timer.
a pointer to a CAN network interface.

a pointer to the absolute time of the next trigger of the timer. If start is NULL, the next trigger time is given by interval with respect to the current time as obtained with can_net_get_time(). If interval is also NULL, the timer is stopped.
a pointer to the interval between successive triggers of the timer. If interval is NULL, the timer only triggers once, at the time given by start.

briefdescription.para: Stops a CAN timer and unregisters it with a network interface.
Starts a CAN timer and registers it with a network interface.

detaileddescription.para: The timer will trigger only once.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN timer.
a pointer to a CAN network interface.
the timeout (in milliseconds) with respect to the current time as obtained with can_net_get_time(). If timeout is negative, the timer is stopped.

briefdescription.para: Returns the alignment (in bytes) of can_recv_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of can_recv_t structure.
Creates a new CAN frame receiver.
Destroys a CAN frame receiver.
Returns a pointer to the allocator used to allocate the CAN frame receiver.
Retrieves the callback function used to process CAN frames with a receiver.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN frame receiver.
the address at which to store a pointer to the callback function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the callback function used to process CAN frames with a receiver.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN frame receiver.
a pointer to the function to be invoked by can_net_recv().
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: Registers a CAN frame receiver with a network interface and starts processing frames.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN frame receiver.
a pointer to a CAN network interface.
the CAN identifier for which the receiver should be invoked.
the flags that should be set in every accepted frame.

briefdescription.para: Stops a CAN frame receiver from processing frames and unregisters it with the network interface.
This header file is part of the CAN library; it contains the CAN network interface declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
